metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state (SQLite/PostgreSQL databases), exposes CRUD APIs for indexers, applications, notifications, and history entities, and manages entity lifecycles (add/update/delete indexers). Classified as stateful-crud.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — application distributed as platform-specific archives with no containerization or cloud compute
top_gaps.impact: Prevents elastic scaling, automated deployment, and operational efficiency

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC exists — all infrastructure is outside the repository
top_gaps.impact: Non-reproducible infrastructure, manual provisioning, no disaster recovery automation

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing instrumented — no OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or trace propagation
top_gaps.impact: Cannot debug performance issues or trace request flows through the system

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent cloud audit logging
top_gaps.impact: No forensic capability, no compliance audit trail for administrative actions

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith with single deployable unit and shared database
top_gaps.impact: Limits independent scaling, deployment velocity, and team autonomy

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (README.md, OpenAPI spec, health check descriptions)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Knowledge agent indexing OpenAPI spec and documentation to answer developer questions about Prowlarr API and troubleshooting
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Would need indexing infrastructure (vector DB) and retrieval agent framework

quick_agent_wins.name: API-aware Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: APP-Q5 >= 2 — versioned REST API at /api/v1/ with OpenAPI 3.0.4 specification
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that discovers and invokes Prowlarr API endpoints as tools for managing indexers, triggering searches, checking health
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: OpenAPI spec exists and is comprehensive; agent can call endpoints directly once API key is configured

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: INF-Q11 >= 2 — comprehensive Azure DevOps pipeline with multi-platform builds
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that triggers builds, checks test results, monitors pipeline status across platform matrix
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Azure DevOps API access needed for agent integration

decomposition_strategy.rationale: APP-Q2=1 indicates tightly-coupled monolith, but internal modules (Indexers, Applications, Notifications, History) are identifiable. Dapper-based Datastore layer provides natural seams.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1, no Dockerfile or container definitions found

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); no commercial DB engines (SQLite + PostgreSQL are open source)

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures; all logic in application layer. Criterion not met.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Application uses only open-source databases (SQLite, PostgreSQL). No commercial database engines detected.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=1 (self-managed SQLite/PostgreSQL), DATA-Q3=2 (version unpinned in deployment)

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads exist; stateful-crud archetype with no streaming/ETL artifacts

triggering_questions.note: Score reflects absence of messaging for downstream sync, but no analytics/streaming workloads exist to modernize

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found. Application has no data processing responsibilities beyond CRUD operations.

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context describes 'Indexer manager/proxy for the *arr suite' with no AI-related signal terms.

findings.title: No Managed Compute
findings.description: All compute is self-managed. Application distributed as platform-specific self-contained .NET 8 archives for 10+ platforms with no cloud compute services.
findings.gap: No managed compute infrastructure. Application runs directly on user-provisioned hardware with no cloud orchestration.
findings.recommendation: Containerize using .NET 8 container support and deploy to Amazon EKS. Create Dockerfile based on existing self-contained publish configuration.

findings.title: Self-Managed Databases
findings.description: All databases are self-managed. SQLite is embedded default; PostgreSQL requires user-provisioned instance. No managed database services referenced.
findings.gap: No managed database services. SQLite provides zero operational features. PostgreSQL usage requires full self-management.
findings.recommendation: Migrate PostgreSQL workloads to Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL. Application already has full PostgreSQL support via Npgsql driver.

findings.title: Hardcoded Workflow Orchestration
findings.description: Multi-step operations coordinated through in-process event handlers and background tasks with no dedicated orchestration service.
findings.gap: No dedicated workflow orchestration. Multi-step operations lack visual management, retry logic, and error handling beyond Polly retries.
findings.recommendation: Consider AWS Step Functions for indexer sync → notification → history → app sync workflow when deploying to cloud.

findings.title: No Managed Messaging
findings.description: No managed messaging or streaming infrastructure. Cross-service state changes to downstream *arr applications handled via synchronous HTTP.
findings.gap: State changes crossing service boundaries use synchronous HTTP with no decoupling, dead letter queue, or guaranteed delivery.
findings.recommendation: Introduce Amazon EventBridge for event-driven communication between Prowlarr and downstream *arr applications.

findings.title: No Network Security Infrastructure
findings.description: No VPC, security groups, NACLs, or network segmentation defined. Application exposes port 9696 directly with permissive CORS.
findings.gap: No network security infrastructure. Direct service exposure with no network segmentation or private subnet isolation.
findings.recommendation: Provision VPC with private subnets for application and database. Use security groups with least-privilege rules. Place ALB or API Gateway in public subnet.

findings.title: No API Entry Point
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or managed entry point. Kestrel web server exposed directly on port 9696.
findings.gap: Direct service exposure without throttling, centralized authentication, request validation, or traffic management.
findings.recommendation: Deploy Amazon API Gateway in front of application for throttling, request validation, and centralized auth.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration. Application enforces single-instance operation and cannot scale horizontally.
findings.gap: Single-instance deployment with no ability to scale based on demand.
findings.recommendation: After containerization and single-instance removal, configure EKS HPA based on request rate and CPU utilization.

findings.title: Inadequate Backup and Recovery
findings.description: Application-level backup only for SQLite (ZIP on local filesystem). PostgreSQL has no backup automation. No PITR or cross-region replication.
findings.gap: No cloud backup services. Local filesystem backups vulnerable to disk failure. PostgreSQL not backed up by application.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL for automated backups with PITR. Use AWS Backup with S3 for cross-region replication.

findings.title: No High Availability
findings.description: Single-instance operation with no multi-AZ deployment, no failover, and no fault isolation. Entire application runs as one process on one host.
findings.gap: Single point of failure with no automatic recovery. Any host failure takes down the entire application.
findings.recommendation: Deploy to EKS with replicas across 2+ AZs. Use Aurora PostgreSQL Multi-AZ for database HA.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists in the repository. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or Kustomize files found.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. Infrastructure changes are non-reproducible, manual, and undocumented in code.
findings.recommendation: Define all infrastructure in AWS CDK or Terraform: VPC, EKS cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL, ECR, API Gateway.

findings.title: Partial CI/CD — No Deployment Automation
findings.description: Comprehensive build and test automation via Azure DevOps (10 stages, multi-platform). But no deployment automation — builds produce archives, deployment is manual.
findings.gap: Build is fully automated but deployment is entirely manual. No deploy stage, no automated rollback, no infrastructure deployment.
findings.recommendation: Add deployment stages targeting AWS. Use CodeDeploy to EKS after container builds. Implement automated rollback on health check failure.

findings.title: Tightly-Coupled Monolith
findings.description: Single deployable binary with all functionality bundled. Single-instance enforcement. All modules share database via BasicRepository with cross-module table access.
findings.gap: No module boundaries enforced. Shared mutable state. Cannot deploy, scale, or update modules independently.
findings.recommendation: Begin Strangler Fig extraction: Notifications service first (lowest coupling), then History, then Indexer Search.

findings.title: Primarily Synchronous Communication
findings.description: Cross-service communication with downstream *arr applications is entirely synchronous HTTP with Polly retry. Some internal async (SignalR, background tasks).
findings.gap: Cross-service state propagation uses synchronous HTTP with no decoupling. Downstream service unavailability causes operation failures.
findings.recommendation: Introduce Amazon EventBridge for cross-service state propagation. Map internal IEventAggregator events to EventBridge events.

findings.title: Inconsistent Long-Running Process Handling
findings.description: Custom TaskManager and command queue for background jobs. Bulk operations may exceed 30s with no status polling API or formal async job pattern.
findings.gap: No formal async job pattern with status polling. Bulk searches/syncs may time out with no progress visibility for API clients.
findings.recommendation: Implement async job pattern with status endpoints. Consider Step Functions for multi-step workflows on AWS.

findings.title: API Versioning — Single Version Only
findings.description: Consistent /api/v1/ URL versioning with dedicated API project. OpenAPI 3.0.4 spec generated. Only v1 exists.
findings.gap: No version deprecation policy or migration strategy documented for future v2.
findings.recommendation: Document version deprecation policy. Consider version sunset headers. Current v1-only is acceptable for maturity.

findings.title: No Dynamic Service Discovery
findings.description: Downstream service endpoints configured via user-provided URLs stored in database. No dynamic service discovery or service mesh.
findings.gap: Hard-coded service endpoints with no dynamic discovery. Service relocation requires manual configuration update.
findings.recommendation: Implement AWS Cloud Map or EKS internal DNS when deploying multiple services to AWS.

findings.title: Local Filesystem Storage for Unstructured Data
findings.description: Backups, logs, and config stored on local filesystem. No S3 or managed object storage.
findings.gap: Data on local filesystem — vulnerable to disk failure and inaccessible for external tooling.
findings.recommendation: Store backups in S3 with lifecycle policies. Ship logs to CloudWatch Logs. Store config in Parameter Store/Secrets Manager.

findings.title: Mostly Centralized Data Access with Minor Gaps
findings.description: Well-structured BasicRepository pattern using Dapper with centralized DbFactory and TableMapper. Some direct SQL in migrations.
findings.gap: Mostly centralized but dual-database abstraction (SQLite + PostgreSQL) adds complexity with separate WhereBuilder implementations.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current pattern. When migrating to Aurora PostgreSQL exclusively, remove SQLite-specific code paths.

findings.title: Database Version Not Pinned in Deployment
findings.description: CI tests against PostgreSQL 14/15. Runtime version check exists. But no version pinning in deployment config (no IaC). PostgreSQL 14 approaching EOL Nov 2026.
findings.gap: No explicit deployment version pinning. PostgreSQL 14 approaching EOL. No documented version-update procedure.
findings.recommendation: Pin engine version in IaC when deploying Aurora. Remove PostgreSQL 14 from CI. Add PostgreSQL 16/17 testing.

findings.title: No Cloud Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent. Application-level NLog with Sentry error tracking. Auth events logged locally. No immutable audit trail.
findings.gap: No cloud audit logging. Application logs are mutable files. No compliance-grade audit mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock. Ship audit logs to CloudWatch Logs.

findings.title: No Encryption at Rest
findings.description: SQLite database files, Config.xml with credentials, and backup ZIPs all stored unencrypted on filesystem. No KMS or encryption mechanism.
findings.gap: All sensitive data at rest is unencrypted.
findings.recommendation: Enable Aurora encryption with customer-managed KMS key. Store backups in S3 with SSE-KMS. Use Secrets Manager for credentials.

findings.title: Static API Key Authentication
findings.description: API key auth via X-Api-Key header/query. Single GUID shared across clients. No OAuth2/JWT. 'None' auth mode allows unauthenticated access.
findings.gap: Static API key without rotation, no per-client keys, no token-based auth. 'None' mode allows open access.
findings.recommendation: Implement API Gateway with OAuth2/JWT authorizers backed by Cognito. Replace static API key with per-client keys.

findings.title: No Centralized Identity Integration
findings.description: Self-managed authentication with PBKDF2 local credentials. No external IdP, no OIDC/SAML, no SSO.
findings.gap: No centralized identity provider integration. Authentication entirely self-contained.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito for OIDC federation and SSO. Use ALB/API Gateway OIDC integration.

findings.title: Filesystem-Based Secrets Without Rotation
findings.description: Secrets in Config.xml (generated at runtime, not in VCS). Overridable via env vars. No external secrets manager. No rotation.
findings.gap: Production credentials in plaintext XML/env vars. No automated rotation. No centralized secrets management.
findings.recommendation: Migrate all secrets to AWS Secrets Manager with automated rotation. Use IAM roles for service-to-service auth.

findings.title: No Compute Hardening or Patching
findings.description: No patching strategy, no vulnerability scanning, no hardened base images. Self-update handles app updates only.
findings.gap: No infrastructure patching. No vulnerability scanning. No container image scanning.
findings.recommendation: Use hardened base images when containerizing. Enable ECR image scanning. Implement SSM Patch Manager for EC2.

findings.title: Limited Security Pipeline
findings.description: SonarCloud SAST in pipeline (no blocking gate). Dependabot monitors devcontainers only — not NuGet or npm. No container/DAST scanning.
findings.gap: Dependabot not monitoring primary ecosystems. SonarCloud without blocking gate. No DAST or container scanning.
findings.recommendation: Expand Dependabot to nuget/npm. Add SonarCloud quality gate. Add container scanning when containerized.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or tracing SDK. Request-level logging with sequence IDs but no trace propagation across service boundaries.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. Cannot trace requests to downstream services. Debugging requires manual log correlation.
findings.recommendation: Instrument with OpenTelemetry .NET SDK. Configure AWS X-Ray as backend. Propagate trace context in HTTP calls.

findings.title: No SLO Definitions
findings.description: No formal SLOs. Internal health checks monitor component status but are not SLO definitions with error budgets.
findings.gap: No SLOs for critical journeys. No error budget tracking. No formal acceptable service level definition.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs: search p99 < 5s, indexer availability > 99.5%, API error rate < 1%. Implement with CloudWatch.

findings.title: No Business Metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics published. Internal analytics stored in DB only. No CloudWatch metrics, Prometheus, or external dashboards.
findings.gap: Cannot measure search success rate, indexer utilization, or business KPIs externally.
findings.recommendation: Instrument CloudWatch custom metrics: searches/min, success rate, indexer response times, sync rate.

findings.title: No Anomaly Detection or External Alerting
findings.description: Internal health checks display in UI only. No CloudWatch alarms, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie, no anomaly detection.
findings.gap: No external alerting. Health issues visible only in application UI. No escalation to operations teams.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch alarms with anomaly detection. Integrate SNS for alert routing to PagerDuty/OpsGenie.

findings.title: No Deployment Strategy
findings.description: Self-update mechanism applies changes in-place. No blue/green, canary, rolling deployments, or traffic shifting.
findings.gap: Direct in-place update with downtime. No staged rollout, no automated rollback, no deployment validation.
findings.recommendation: Implement rolling deployments on EKS. Progress to blue/green via CodeDeploy with automatic rollback.

findings.title: Limited Integration Test Coverage
findings.description: Integration tests exist against SQLite/PostgreSQL in CI. API endpoint validation present. But critical business workflows and contract tests missing.
findings.gap: No end-to-end tests for critical journeys. No contract tests with downstream services. Selenium tests are brittle.
findings.recommendation: Expand integration tests to cover critical workflows. Add contract tests for downstream API consumers.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no self-healing, no SSM Automation. Only auto-recovery is corrupt log database restart.
findings.gap: All incident response is manual and user-initiated. No automated remediation or runbooks.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common failures. Implement SSM Automation documents. Configure health-check-driven pod restarts on EKS.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: No dashboards, no CODEOWNERS for monitoring, no named alarm owners, no SLO team attribution.
findings.gap: No observability ownership. No dashboards. No alarm ownership. No team attribution.
findings.recommendation: Create CloudWatch dashboards per functional area. Define alarm owners. Add CODEOWNERS for monitoring config.

findings.title: No Resource Tagging Governance
findings.description: No AWS resources exist, so no tagging. No existing tagging standard for when cloud resources are provisioned.
findings.gap: No tagging governance. No cost allocation, ownership, or environment identification tags.
findings.recommendation: Establish tagging standard (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter) before provisioning AWS resources. Enforce via Config rules.
